1.solution: From x/y+y/x=17: x²+y²=17xy. So x²+18y²=17xy+17y²=17y(x+y). Substitute into eq1: (x+y)+17y(x+y)=0 → (x+y)(1+17y)=0. Since x+y≠0: y=−1/17. |102y|=|102×(−1/17)|=6.

3.solution: Monic quadratic with roots 5,7: f(x)=(x−5)(x−7)=x²−12x+35. Minimum at vertex x=(5+7)/2=6: f(6)=(6−5)(6−7)=1×(−1)=−1.

4.solution: Cond1: x≥0. Cond2: x²−12x+31≥0 → x≤6−√5 or x≥6+√5. Cond3: x²≤49 → x≤7 (with x≥0). Since 6+√5≈8.24>7, upper branch excluded. Intersection: x∈[0,6−√5]. Endpoints included (all conditions satisfied at equality). Answer: C.

5.solution: x²+3nx+4n=0: discriminant=9n²−16n≥0 → n(9n−16)≥0 → n≤0 or n≥16/9≈1.78. Non-neg integers <20: n=0 satisfies n≤0; n=2,3,...,19 satisfy n≥2>16/9. n=1 excluded (disc<0). Count: 1+18=19.

6.solution: Roots 5n,4n: sum=9n=−b→b=−9n; product=20n²=c. Discriminant=b²−4c=81n²−80n²=n². For smallest 3-digit value: n²≥100→n≥10 (positive integer). At n=10: discriminant=100. Answer: 100.

7.solution: By AM-GM: ab+cd≥2√(abcd) and ac+bd≥2√(abcd). Product: (ab+cd)(ac+bd)≥4abcd. Equality requires ab=cd AND ac=bd → a=c, contradicting distinct values. So strictly >4abcd. Answer: C (>4abcd).

8.solution: Let a=14−x, b=x−4 with a+b=10, a,b≥0. Maximize a³b². By weighted AM-GM: optimal at a/3=b/2=k → a=6,b=4 (9k=... wait: 3k+2k=5k=10→k=2). Max=(14−x)³(x−4)²=6³×4²=216×16=3456 at x=8.

10.solution: |x²−3x−1|<3(x²+x+1) (denominator always>0). Right ineq: x²−3x−1<3x²+3x+3 → 2(x+1)(x+2)>0 → x<−2 or x>−1. Left ineq: −3x²−3x−3<x²−3x−1 → 4x²+2>0 → always true. Solution: (−∞,−2)∪(−1,∞). Answer: A.
